Gene_Name.reason: Nramp3 is explicitly listed in Table 1 and analyzed in Results/Fig. 3D.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper defines Nramp as 'natural resistance-associated macrophage protein' and describes Nramp3 as an Fe2+ transporter, supporting classification as an Nramp family metal transporter.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Iron accumulation was observed in shoots/leaves, while roots showed no obvious difference among treatments.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The Results state that Nramp3 'can transport Fe2+ and is upregulated by iron starvation.'

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Fig. 3D/Results state that 20–100 mg/L γ-Fe2O3 NPs lowered Nramp3 expression versus control, while 100 mg/L Fe3+ and Fe(II)-EDTA showed much higher expression.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: The Results explicitly state that Nramp3 protein 'localizes in the vacuolar membrane.'

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The Discussion states that lower Nramp3 expression under all γ-Fe2O3 NP concentrations indicated the plant was in iron-sufficient status.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: The paper measured gene expression by RT-PCR after foliar treatments and measured iron content by atomic absorption spectrophotometry.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The Discussion explicitly states that much higher Nramp3 expression under 100 mg/L Fe3+ and Fe(II)-EDTA suggested these treatments could not alleviate iron deficiency effectively via foliar spray.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: The paper describes wax as composed of long-chain aliphatic hydrocarbons derived from very-long-chain fatty acids, supporting classification as lipid-related.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper states wax is a protective material on the leaf epidermis and plays an important role in particle incorporation.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The paper places cuticular wax on the leaf epidermis/cuticle.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results and Discussion explicitly note that ABCG12 was lower or unchanged even when wax content increased, supporting the conclusion that wax transport/accumulation is multigenic and complex.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: ABCG12 expression was measured by RT-PCR, and wax loads were measured by chloroform extraction and gravimetric weighing.
